#### Short description:  {#short-description:}

Reduce lower back pain

#### Introduction: {#introduction:}

Intro Video & thumbnail: 

| Video Link | Video Thumbnail |
| :---- | :---- |
| [https://drive.google.com/file/d/1WUypKkzv3SB8mXAMuLlWY-Zc-Qi9PeJ3/view?usp=drive\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1WUypKkzv3SB8mXAMuLlWY-Zc-Qi9PeJ3/view?usp=drive_link) | [Lower Back Intro Video Thumbnail.jpg](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1rUzK74XeanxePyL4MWRxIAgk_-UJSEER/view?usp=drive_link) |

Welcome to the evidence-based Lower Back Care program. This therapeutic method is proven to reduce and prevent lower back pain. 

#### What you’ll learn:  {#what-you’ll-learn:}

During this program you will learn the causes of most lower back pain and build skills to reduce and prevent your pain.

#### 

#### Who is this for: {#who-is-this-for:}

This program can help most people with lower back pain, especially if it is your first occurrence or if you have pain or stiffness from time to time.

\<Talk to your doctor structural content goes here\> 

### ---

---

### 

### Session 1  {#session-1}

**Session title:** 

#### Getting Started {#getting-started}

**Session attribute:**   
Session 1

**Session description:**   
Learn the contributing factors for most lower back pain, assess your pain and set goals.

##### 

##### S1M1 {#s1m1}

**Module type:**

Education

**Audio:**  
Yes

#### 

**Module title:** 

###### Introduction {#introduction}

#### 

**Body:** 

You are not alone. Back pain is very common. According to the World Health Organization (WHO), 619 million people—about 1 in 13 people—experienced lower back pain in 2020\. Cases of back pain are expected to increase to an estimated 843 million by 2050\.

(headline) Who benefits most from this program?

This program can help most people with lower back pain and stiffness, especially if this is your first time experiencing back pain or stiffness, or if you have occasional or recurring pain or stiffness.

This program may be particularly helpful if you answer “yes” to any of the following questions:

* Are there times when your back pain or stiffness lessens or goes away?  
* Is your movement limited in any direction when you do have back pain or stiffness?  
* Does your pain or stiffness feel more intense in the morning but improve after moving or walking around?

(headline) Talk to your healthcare provider

Consult with your healthcare provider before beginning this program, especially if: 

* Your pain is intense and/or constant.   
* You can’t lie flat on your stomach.  
* You can’t stand upright at all.  
* Your symptoms do not improve after this program.  
* Your symptoms get worse after a couple of days.  
* You have numbness, tingling or weakness in your legs.

\<button\> Mark as done

---

##### S1M2 {#s1m2}

**Module type:**

Care Plan

**Audio:**  
No

#### 

**Module title:** 

###### Set Goals {#set-goals}

#### 

**Body:** 

The program objective is to reduce or eliminate lower back pain or stiffness. Answer the following questions to set goals and track your progress toward them.

\<button\> Create plan

**Question page:**

When you experience lower back pain or stiffness, what is the level of that pain on a scale of 0-10? (0-no pain, 10-severe pain)  
\[Number with validation 28\]

What are your goals? (select all that apply)

* Reduce pain  
* Prevent future pain  
* Increase range of motion  
* Perform an activity without discomfort  
* Other

\[Multiple choice, multi-select\]

If other, what is your goal or goals? (optional)  
\[open answer with validation 26\]

---

##### S1M3 {#s1m3}

**Module type:**

Education

**Audio:**  
Yes

**Module title:** 

###### Causes of Back Pain  {#causes-of-back-pain}

**Body:** 

Most back pain occurs in the lower back. Between the bones in the spine, there are soft tissues made up of cartilage and ligaments. Pain and stiffness can occur when these tissues are overstretched or forced out of place. 

(headline) Pain and your posture

A common and easily corrected cause of lower back pain is incorrect posture while standing, sitting, bending and lifting. Incorrect posture is usually the result of bending or slouching forward for too long, which can overstretch your lower back and disc ligaments. Lower back pain and stiffness can be prevented by practicing correct posture.

(headline) Skill building

You will build skills in this program to correct shifted back tissues and prevent postures that can overstretch your lower back. (Sometimes we recommend an “overcorrected” position to help you learn the correct one.)

(headline) Key insights

1. Most back pain occurs in the lower back due to overstretched or damaged tissues.  
2. Incorrect posture while sitting, standing, bending and lifting can result in overstretched lower back tissues.   
3. The exercises you will learn in this program can reduce and even eliminate your back discomfort. 

\<button\>  Mark as done

---

##### S1M4 {#s1m4}

**Module type:**

Assessment

**Audio:**  
No

#### 

**Module title:** 

#### Assess Your Discomfort {#assess-your-discomfort}

#### 

**Body:** 

Identify your current level of discomfort based on pain you feel in your lower back. This will help you assess your improvement.

\<button\> Take assessment

**Question page:**

How difficult do you find it today to **get out of bed?**

* Not difficult at all (0)  
* Minimally difficult (1)  
* Somewhat difficult (2)  
* Fairly difficult (3)  
* Very difficult (4)  
* Unable to do (5)

\[multiple choice, choose one\]

### 

### Session 2  {#session-2}

**Session title:** 

#### Sitting Posture {#sitting-posture}

**Session attribute:**   
Session 2

**Session description:**   
Practice the best sitting posture to reduce and prevent pain.

##### S2M1 {#s2m1}

**Module type:**

Education

**Audio:**  
Yes

#### 

**Module title:** 

###### Sitting and Back Pain {#sitting-and-back-pain}

#### 

**Body:** 

Incorrect sitting posture commonly causes lower back pain. And once lower back pain develops, a misaligned sitting position can make it worse.

When we sit for prolonged periods of time, the muscles that support the lower back become fatigued and relax. Our body sags, resulting in a slouched posture. Slouching over time can cause pain by overstretching ligaments.  
![][image1]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1j4Xomr9mFf4KaOo0jBq-LuOGiD8vXRt8/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1j4Xomr9mFf4KaOo0jBq-LuOGiD8vXRt8/view?usp=share_link)

People with sedentary office jobs can easily develop lower back pain if they sit in a slouched position for hours. Periodic standing and walking can help alleviate this.

When sitting for a long time, try placing a cushion or small pillow behind your lower back for support. Take breaks once an hour to stand up and walk for a few minutes.

Corrections in posture can reduce and even eliminate back pain in some cases. The key is to practice posture that avoids bending too far forward or backward.

(headline) Key insights

1. Incorrect sitting posture, such as slouching forward or bending backward, is a common cause for lower back pain.  
2. Practicing proper sitting posture can improve or eliminate lower back discomfort and pain.  
3. Use a cushion behind your lower back for support when sitting for longer periods of time.  
4. Take breaks every hour to stand, bend backwards 5 or 6 times and walk around.

\<button\> Mark as done

---

##### 

##### S2M2 {#s2m2}

**Module type:**

Physical Exercises

**Audio:**  
Yes

#### 

**\# Required:**  
1

**Tracking content:** 

Log at least 1 day

**Module title:** 

###### Sitting Technique {#sitting-technique}

#### 

**Body:** 

This technique can help you achieve a sitting posture that reduces and prevents lower back pain.   
![][image2]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1cEIGQE81jrU8Gx5u3F\_ooFg6RPBdeZ\_S/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1cEIGQE81jrU8Gx5u3F_ooFg6RPBdeZ_S/view?usp=share_link)

1. Extend yourself upward, sitting as tall as you can for a few seconds.  
2. Relax \- releasing a little bit of the strain.

![][image3]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1J6NG96r6KFUHnGqqnh90Af8p4psU7UNc/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1J6NG96r6KFUHnGqqnh90Af8p4psU7UNc/view?usp=share_link)

(headline) Schedule

* Do it 3 times today.   
* Continue practicing every day until it becomes a habit.

![][image4]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1bR37dah9OZoARoWIgxDEwE5AGo6DuB40/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1bR37dah9OZoARoWIgxDEwE5AGo6DuB40/view?usp=share_link)  
(headline) Tip  
A lumbar roll or cushion helps you maintain correct sitting posture. Sit with your buttocks all the way back, bend forward and position the cushion in your lower back.

\<button\> Log activity

**Question page:**  
Date:   
\[date picker with validation 40 & 43\]

How many times did you do this exercise?

* 1  
* 2  
* 3  
* More than 3

\[Multiple choice, single-select\]

Performing this exercise resulted in: (select all that apply)

* Increasing range of motion  
* Reducing discomfort  
* Moving discomfort from lower limbs to back  
* Slightly increasing discomfort  
* Significantly increasing discomfort  
* Other

\[Multiple choice, multi-select\]

If other, what did you experience? (optional)  
\[open answer with validation 26\]

---

### Session 3 {#session-3}

**Session title:** 

#### Standing and Lifting {#standing-and-lifting}

**Session attribute:**   
Session 3

**Session description:**   
Learn and practice standing posture and lifting techniques that reduce lower back pain.

##### S3M1 {#s3m1}

**Module type:**

Education

**Audio:**  
Yes

#### 

**Module title:** 

###### Standing and Lifting Safely {#standing-and-lifting-safely}

#### 

**Body:** 

(headline) Standing  
With prolonged standing, your lower back muscles can become fatigued, which can result in an exaggerated arch in your lower back and cause pain. To stand correctly, begin in a standing position, exaggerate the arch in your lower back and then reduce it a little bit.

(headline) Lifting  
![][image5]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1j4ApYWWBDD4xdTIvPhUfhtdy1gFRwUdp/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1j4ApYWWBDD4xdTIvPhUfhtdy1gFRwUdp/view?usp=share_link)

Lifting with your back instead of your hip and leg muscles can lead to back pain and injuries. To lift safely, bend your legs into a squat position. Maintain the natural arch in your lower back. Lift with your hip and leg muscles — not your back muscles. 

Avoid twisting as you lift. First lift, then turn yourself if needed. If you are lifting many objects, take frequent breaks. Avoid lifting if you are experiencing back pain.  
   
\<button\> Mark as done

---

##### S3M2 {#s3m2}

**Module type:**

Physical Exercises

**Audio:**  
Yes

**\# Required:**

1

**Tracking content:** 

Log at least 1 day

**Module title:** 

###### Standing Posture {#standing-posture}

**Body:**

This technique gives you a standing posture that protects your back. ![][image6]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1KRrPf8-hyTbSfCsTon-IaJr4gFeC2HYx/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1KRrPf8-hyTbSfCsTon-IaJr4gFeC2HYx/view?usp=share_link)

1. Stand up straight with a hand on your lower back arch.

![][image7]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1EH9wlTVYzchPRmBdcOBQi\_z4zRKKVCgg/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1EH9wlTVYzchPRmBdcOBQi_z4zRKKVCgg/view?usp=share_link)

2. Stand as tall as you can, reaching up with your hands and being careful not to arch or round your back.

![][image8]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1oZLeEPLpS3ZuueyOf-6NlMRTP3IaVP1m/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1oZLeEPLpS3ZuueyOf-6NlMRTP3IaVP1m/view?usp=share_link)

3. Now bring your arms down to your sides and relax your shoulders while maintaining your back posture.

(headline) Schedule

* Do it 3 times today.   
* Continue practicing every day until it becomes a habit.

\<button\> Log activity

**Question page:**

Date:   
\[date picker with validation 40 & 43\]

How many times did you do this exercise?

* 1  
* 2  
* 3  
* More than 3

\[Multiple choice, single-select\]

Performing this exercise resulted in: (select all that apply)

* Increasing range of motion  
* Reducing discomfort  
* Moving discomfort from lower limbs to back  
* Slightly increasing discomfort  
* Significantly increasing discomfort  
* Other

\[Multiple choice, multi-select\]

If other, what did you experience? (optional)  
\[open answer with validation 26\]

---

##### S3M3 {#s3m3}

**Module type:**

Physical Exercises

**Audio:**  
Yes

**\# Required:**

1

**Tracking content:** 

Log 1 day

**Module title:** 

#### Lifting Technique {#lifting-technique}

#### 

**Body:** 

This technique helps you lift objects without injury to your lower back. Choose something small to start. Your goal is to lift objects this way every time.  
![][image9]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1q7EO4fM4S5WG4WYFl64ZlZ9l7aXIPV23/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1q7EO4fM4S5WG4WYFl64ZlZ9l7aXIPV23/view?usp=share_link)

1. Stand up close to the object, feet wider than your shoulders, while maintaining the natural arch in your back.

![][image10]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Ysnj8qibx4GimiLG7MmD0voA1vzu3nIa/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Ysnj8qibx4GimiLG7MmD0voA1vzu3nIa/view?usp=share_link)

2. Bend your knees to pick up the object — still maintaining the arch in your back. Hold the object as close to yourself as possible. Keep your weight even between your feet.

![][image11]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1sOvDeTEyMsGRnIq4\_Bxumm90elp0TPUN/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1sOvDeTEyMsGRnIq4_Bxumm90elp0TPUN/view?usp=share_link)

3. Lean back, extending your buttocks backward. Lift with your legs and hips, not your back. Lift steadily rather than quickly. Avoid twisting as you lift. First lift, then turn yourself if needed.

![][image12]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1KpxovJmBsXfEtt9QJcWjJkSvtI980hyl/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1KpxovJmBsXfEtt9QJcWjJkSvtI980hyl/view?usp=share_link)

(headline) Schedule

* Do it 3 times today.   
* Continue using this method every time you lift an object.

\<button\> Log activity

#### 

**Question page:**

Date:   
\[date picker with validation 40 & 43\]

How many times did you use this lifting technique?

* 1  
* 2  
* 3  
* More than 3

\[Multiple choice, single-select\]

Performing this lifting technique resulted in: (select all that apply)

* Increasing range of motion  
* Reducing discomfort  
* Moving discomfort from lower limbs to back  
* Slightly increasing discomfort  
* Significantly increasing discomfort  
* Other

\[Multiple choice, multi-select\]

If other, what did you experience? (optional)  
\[open answer with validation 26\]

---

### Session 4 {#session-4}

**Session title:** 

#### Backward Bending {#backward-bending}

**Session attribute:**   
Session 4

**Session description:**   
Learn to reduce lower back discomfort with backward bending exercises.

##### S4M1 {#s4m1}

**Module type:**

Education

**Audio:**  
Yes

#### 

**Module title:** 

###### When Backward Bending Is Helping {#when-backward-bending-is-helping}

#### 

**Body:** 

These backward bending exercises are designed to reduce discomfort and improve your range of motion. They are not meant to strengthen muscles. 

These exercises are helping your back: 

* If your discomfort moves from your leg, buttock or side to the middle of your lower back.  
* If your discomfort gradually reduces in intensity.  
* If your range of motion for your back increases.

Stay relaxed and perform the motions until you feel slight resistance or discomfort. This can help your range of motion increase. If you have acute or sharp pain, consult your healthcare provider on when to start exercising your back. 

(headline) Key insights

1. Backward bending exercises are designed to reduce discomfort, not to increase strength.  
2. The exercises are successful if they help reduce your pain or centralize it to the middle of your lower back.  
3. If you have acute pain, consult your healthcare provider.

\<button\> Mark as done

---

##### S4M2 {#s4m2}

**Module type:**

Education

**Audio:**  
Yes

#### 

**Module title:** 

###### Why Bend Backwards {#why-bend-backwards}

**Body:** 

Many jobs and activities of daily living require prolonged bending over or stooping forward, such as working at a computer and driving a car. This puts stress on your lower back. Bending backward relieves this stress by providing an opposite action. 

Backward bending exercises can reduce lower back discomfort and pain by correcting the soft tissues in your lower back that have moved out of position or that have distorted in shape.

\<button\> Mark as done

---

##### S4M3 {#s4m3}

**Module type:**

Evaluate

**Audio:**  
Yes

**Module title:** 

###### Assess Your Range of Motion {#assess-your-range-of-motion}

**Body:** 

Before you begin the backward bending exercises, assess your back’s range of motion:

1. Stand with your side facing a mirror. Stand with correct posture — standing up tall and maintaining the natural arch in your lower back.   
2. Slide your hands slowly down the front of your legs and see how far forward you can comfortably bend. This is your forward bending range of motion.

![][image13]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1p9m\_N-iM9alfUN\_3JD0Zw1dl-UNbaYgl/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1p9m_N-iM9alfUN_3JD0Zw1dl-UNbaYgl/view?usp=share_link)

3. Return to the standing position.  
4. Place your hands in the arch in your lower back and slowly bend backwards, going as far as you can comfortably bend backwards. This is your backward bending range of motion. 

![][image14]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1FFFkZHCnVo\_Ncd-0PD4sXWcVnG2aCJrh/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1FFFkZHCnVo_Ncd-0PD4sXWcVnG2aCJrh/view?usp=share_link)

You know you have reached your full range of motion when you feel restricted bending in either direction, meaning you feel some tightness or mild discomfort. As you continue to practice the techniques in this program, your range of motion will likely increase over time.

(headline) Schedule

* Log 1 time today.  
* Repeat as needed to check your range of motion and measure improvement.

\<button\> Assess progress

**Question page:**

Date:  
\[date picker with validation 40 & 43\]

Notes and observations: (optional)  
\[open answer with validation 26\]

##### ---

##### S4M4 {#s4m4}

**Module type:**

Physical Exercises

**Audio:**  
Yes

**\# Required:**  
1

**Tracking content:**  
Log 1 time, repeat as needed

#### 

**Module title:** 

###### Relax Your Back {#relax-your-back}

#### 

**Body:** 

It’s important to relax your back each time before starting a backward bending exercise. The relaxation helps improve your range of motion.

If you have any significant pain in your back while lying down on your stomach, wait until the pain subsides before doing any back exercises.

![][image15]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1UyYICuAE3\_JA8HS4kx4nL95Z4QLWt8oZ/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1UyYICuAE3_JA8HS4kx4nL95Z4QLWt8oZ/view?usp=share_link)

1. Lie on your stomach on a firm surface, like the ground, with your head facing to one side. Then relax your abdomen, back and legs completely to remove added tension in your lower back.   
2. Inhale and slowly exhale while relaxing your back and abdominal muscles.   
3. Continue breathing and let your abdomen slowly sag into the firm surface. Stay in this position for 3-5 minutes.

(headline) Schedule

* Log 1 time today.  
* Repeat as needed, especially at the onset of pain.

(headline) Tip  
You may feel less pain in your lower back when you lie down on your stomach and relax it. This can also provide relief if you have a sudden episode of back pain. 

\<button\> Log activity

**Question page:**

Date:  
\[date picker with validation 40 & 43\]

Performing this exercise resulted in: (select all that apply)

* Increasing range of motion  
* Reducing discomfort  
* Moving discomfort from lower limbs to back  
* Slightly increasing discomfort  
* Significantly increasing discomfort  
* Other

\[Multiple choice, multi-select\]

If other, what did you experience? (optional)  
\[open answer with validation 26\]

Notes and observations: (optional)  
\[open answer with validation 26\]

##### 

##### S4M5 {#s4m5}

**Module type:**

Physical Exercises

**Audio:**  
Yes

**\# Required:**

1

**Tracking content:** 

Log at least 1 day

**Module title:** 

###### Press Up to Elbows {#press-up-to-elbows}

**Body:** 

![][image16]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1cFiBoPUQYhumb8TUK0bTt6UZRmpo-YVM/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1cFiBoPUQYhumb8TUK0bTt6UZRmpo-YVM/view?usp=share_link)

1. Lie face down on the floor and place your hands under your shoulders. Press your hands into the floor, lifting your head and torso upwards.

![][image17]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1lm9gaIj7FUzvn1FIkQZzb1XjicGCBPyL/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1lm9gaIj7FUzvn1FIkQZzb1XjicGCBPyL/view?usp=share_link)

2. If you can, press up further until you can rest on your elbows. If not, then gradually work your way up to pressing to your elbows over time.   
3. Hold your position for 2-3 minutes.

![][image18]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1hXM5QynqJPGb3rUlojhuS7nw3QUikj9T/view](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1hXM5QynqJPGb3rUlojhuS7nw3QUikj9T/view)

(headline) Schedule

* Do this exercise 3-5 times today.   
* Repeat 3-5 times daily until you feel ready to move on to the press up to straight arms.

\<button\> Log activity

* 2  
* 3  
* 4  
* 5

##### S4M6 {#s4m6}

**Module type:**

Physical Exercises

**Audio:**  
Yes

**\# Required:**   
1

**Tracking content:** 

Log at least 1 day

**Module title:** 

###### Press Up to Straight Arms {#press-up-to-straight-arms}

**Body:**   
Now that you feel comfortable pressing up to elbows, you are ready to try pressing up to straight arms. If you feel pain or discomfort, you can return to the press up to elbows.

![][image19]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1psogkQMHcmL5vNtX6IbVNdJBiidOnmQq/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1psogkQMHcmL5vNtX6IbVNdJBiidOnmQq/view?usp=share_link)

1. Lie face down and place your hands under your shoulders. Begin to straighten your arms — pushing the top half of your body upwards. Gradually straighten your arms as far as your range of motion permits. 

![][image20]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/17a\_0w5-iopkr8MKhPQ29KJ7Rp7RWMYHS/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/17a_0w5-iopkr8MKhPQ29KJ7Rp7RWMYHS/view?usp=share_link)

2. Hold 1-2 seconds, then return to the starting position.  
3. Repeat this movement and try to raise your upper body a little higher each time so you end up with your arms as straight as possible. Repeat until you reach your full range of motion.

![][image21]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1gSzfh3DPK88rdguGh22rksgra811Zmco/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1gSzfh3DPK88rdguGh22rksgra811Zmco/view?usp=share_link)

(headline) Schedule

* Do this 3 times today.   
* Repeat 3 times daily until you feel ready to move on.

(headline) Tip  
Keep your pelvis, hips and legs relaxed and allow your lower back to sag toward the floor. 

\<button\> Log activity

##### S4M7 {#s4m7}

**Module type:**

Physical Exercises

**Audio:**  
Yes

**\# Required:**   
1

**Tracking content:** 

Log at least 1 day

**Module title:** 

###### Standing Backward Bend {#standing-backward-bend}

#### 

**Body:**   
![][image22]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1KRrPf8-hyTbSfCsTon-IaJr4gFeC2HYx/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1KRrPf8-hyTbSfCsTon-IaJr4gFeC2HYx/view?usp=share_link)

1. Stand upright with your feet slightly apart. Place your hands in the arch in your back with your fingers pointing downward.

![][image23]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1YXwtKan9hAUe3Ty7t9KwlxKdC4Gs2w7w/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1YXwtKan9hAUe3Ty7t9KwlxKdC4Gs2w7w/view?usp=share_link)

2. Bend backwards at the waist — using your hands as a pivot point and support.  
3. Hold 1-2 seconds. Then return to the starting position.  
4. Repeat until you reach your full range of motion.

![][image24]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1kkEeuS-ayUrUtzHlUBzpHivTubr6z0bR/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1kkEeuS-ayUrUtzHlUBzpHivTubr6z0bR/view?usp=share_link)

(headline) Schedule

* Do this 3 times today.  
* Repeat as needed.

(headline) Tip  
After sitting or bending forward for a period of time, stand and lean backwards as much as comfortable to help to prevent lower back discomfort.

\<button\> Log activity

##### Session 5 {#session-5}

#### 

**Session title:** 

###### Forward Bending {#forward-bending}

**Session description:** 

Practice forward bending exercises to stretch the lower back tendons and muscles.

##### S5M1 {#s5m1}

**Module type:**

Education

**Audio:**  
Yes

#### 

**Module title:** 

###### Forward Bending Exercises {#forward-bending-exercises}

#### 

**Body:** 

Forward bending exercises are the opposite of backward bending exercises. Bending forward stretches the lower back tendons and muscles.   
![][image25]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1p9m\_N-iM9alfUN\_3JD0Zw1dl-UNbaYgl/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1p9m_N-iM9alfUN_3JD0Zw1dl-UNbaYgl/view?usp=share_link)

Always follow forward bending exercises with backward bending exercises to maintain a balanced range of motion.

(headline) Caution with forward bends  
If forward bending exercises increase your pain or stiffness, then that is a signal from your body that you should stop them right away. 

(headline) Key insights

1. Forward bending exercises help you have a balanced range of motion for your back.  
2. Always follow forward bending exercises with backward bending exercises.  
3. If bending forward increases your back discomfort, then stop right away.

\<button\> Mark as done

---

#### 

### S5M2 {#s5m2}

**Module type:**

Physical Exercises

**Audio:**  
Yes

#### 

**\# Required:**  
1

**Tracking content:**

Log at least 1 day

**Module title:** 

Lying Forward Bend

#### 

**Body:**   
![][image26]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1nu7RZ0ofu7HmVLRVRmxwZgxHICsYTNVO/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1nu7RZ0ofu7HmVLRVRmxwZgxHICsYTNVO/view?usp=share_link)

1. Lie on your back with your knees bent and your feet flat on the floor. 

![][image27]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1e4s74QTwX51H6BSVrsPOn0BcDrqYgcSM/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1e4s74QTwX51H6BSVrsPOn0BcDrqYgcSM/view?usp=share_link)

2. Gently bring both knees up toward your chest, until you feel tightness.  
3. Maintain this position for a few seconds. Then, lower your legs to the starting position.   
4. Repeat this motion 3-5 times — That is 1 set. Your goal is to bring your knees closer to your chest each time.

![][image28]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/12-586vWV8KSBzDYQ6J7WdL2wT1ZAF7Ka/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/12-586vWV8KSBzDYQ6J7WdL2wT1ZAF7Ka/view?usp=share_link)

(headline) Schedule

* Do 3-5 sets of this exercise today.   
* Repeat daily as needed to relieve discomfort or to reach full range of motion.

\<button\> Log activity

##### S5M3 {#s5m3}

**Module type:**

Physical Exercises

**Audio:**  
Yes

**\# Required:**

1

**Tracking content:**

Log at least 1 day

**Module title:** 

###### Standing Forward Bend {#standing-forward-bend}

**Body:**   
![][image29]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/16tbR5T5esrKUvR2272asliGtSL1F5oqO/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/16tbR5T5esrKUvR2272asliGtSL1F5oqO/view?usp=share_link)

1. Stand upright with your feet shoulder width apart. Allow your hands to hang comfortably by your side.

![][image30]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1f0xDkvzaRb0BjMFLDqF3RFaJ2dOmeuYj/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1f0xDkvzaRb0BjMFLDqF3RFaJ2dOmeuYj/view?usp=share_link)

2. Bend forward at the waist, reaching as far toward the ground as possible.   
3. Repeat this motion 3-5 times, bringing your hands a bit closer to the floor with each movement until you start to feel tightness in your movement. That is 1 set.

![][image31]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1M4INA1iwNd-bNulYrMxbhTCyxFrL5Nsa/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1M4INA1iwNd-bNulYrMxbhTCyxFrL5Nsa/view?usp=share_link)

(headline) Schedule

* Do 1-3 sets of this exercise today.   
* Repeat daily until you reach your full range of motion.

### Session 6 {#session-6}

**Session title:** 

#### Supporting Stretches {#supporting-stretches}

**Session attribute:**   
Session 6

**Session description:**   
Stretch these other areas of your body to maintain a healthy back and prevent future discomfort.

##### S6M1 {#s6m1}

**Module type:**

Education

**Audio:**  
Yes

#### 

**Module title:** 

###### Why Stretch? {#why-stretch?}

#### 

**Body:** 

Reduce lower back pain and prevent future tightness and discomfort by stretching your hamstrings, quadriceps and calves. These muscles support your back as you do many of life’s daily activities, especially moving and lifting objects. 

(headline) Hamstring stretch  
The hamstring muscle is in the back of your thigh. It extends up to your hips and flexes your knees. Sitting for extended periods can shorten them. Stretching your hamstrings can make them stronger, longer and more flexible to better support your back. 

(headline) Quadricep stretch  
Stretching your quadriceps, the muscles along the front of your thighs, helps you position yourself to squat correctly while lifting.

(headline) Calf stretch  
Your calf is made up of 2 muscles along the back of your lower leg. Flexibility in your calf muscles is important for stability and movement, especially when you are squatting properly to safely lift objects off the ground.

\<button\> Mark as done

---

##### S6M2 {#s6m2}

**Module type:**

Physical Exercises

**Audio:**  
Yes

#### 

**\#Required:**  
1

**Tracking content:**

Log at least 1 day

**Module title:** 

###### Hamstring Stretch  {#hamstring-stretch}

#### 

**Body:** 

1. Stand upright and place your hand on the natural arch in your lower back.

![][image32]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1rbpCwzmARnLX39j3IQKFG0J8pu2ISVt8/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1rbpCwzmARnLX39j3IQKFG0J8pu2ISVt8/view?usp=share_link)

2. Place one foot on an elevated surface in front of you 10-25 inches high, like a stool or chair. Choose a height that allows you to keep your elevated leg straight comfortably.

![][image33]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Ot6f\_qSERJCVOhVdeUQemBoi5Sk9OGyd/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Ot6f_qSERJCVOhVdeUQemBoi5Sk9OGyd/view?usp=share_link)

3. Standing tall, keeping your leg straight with your curve still in your lower back, gently lean forward. You should feel a stretch in the back of your thigh.  
4. Relax, breathe and count to 30\. Alternate sides and repeat each side 3 times — That is 1 set.

![][image34]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1MtA6U-JNUPxaoTl5cOHhhtD1YeR2DMKz/view?usp=sharing](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1MtA6U-JNUPxaoTl5cOHhhtD1YeR2DMKz/view?usp=sharing)

(headline) Schedule

* Do 1-3 sets of this exercise today.   
* Repeat 2-3 times daily for optimal results.

\<button\> Log activity

##### S6M3 {#s6m3}

**Module type:**

Physical Exercises

**Audio:**  
Yes

#### 

**\# Required:**  
1

**Tracking content:**  
Log at least 1 day

**Module title:** 

###### Quadricep Stretch {#quadricep-stretch}

#### 

**Body:**   
![][image35]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1wjlXjlLq\_5GQN3hK11PZ5027ta47Tf0d/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1wjlXjlLq_5GQN3hK11PZ5027ta47Tf0d/view?usp=share_link)

1. Facing forward and standing up tall, grab your foot behind your back.  
2. Bring your heel toward your buttocks. Use your core muscles to bring your knee down next to the other knee. You'll feel the stretch in the front of the thigh.  
3. Hold for 30 seconds. Then, repeat with the other leg. That is 1 set.

![][image36]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/12Y9pvcuHXLU6RcsTy4V9EAJl4WTnD-iR/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/12Y9pvcuHXLU6RcsTy4V9EAJl4WTnD-iR/view?usp=share_link)

(headline) Schedule

* Do 1-3 sets of this exercise today.   
* Repeat daily for optimal results.

![][image37]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1ahnBuXoauyPe\_q3\_KfOqsemjGdy98Auo/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1ahnBuXoauyPe_q3_KfOqsemjGdy98Auo/view?usp=share_link)

(headline) Tip  
If it’s difficult to grab your foot, then you can use a chair or counter to support your hands. You can also place a chair behind you and rest your foot on the chair seat.

\<button\> Log activity

##### S6M4 {#s6m4}

**Module type:**

Physical Exercises

**Audio:**  
Yes

**\# Required:**  
1

**Tracking content:**  
Log at least 1 day

**Module title:** 

###### Calf Stretch {#calf-stretch}

#### 

#### **Body:**  {#body:}

![][image38]

[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1sK9I9F2XzdV6QeKKT8aRgsx9NOqXo9AE/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1sK9I9F2XzdV6QeKKT8aRgsx9NOqXo9AE/view?usp=share_link)

1. Stand facing a wall. Place one foot in front of the other, with a slight bend in the front knee. Keep the back leg straight and your heel flat on the ground. Place your hands on the wall for support.

![][image39]

[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1sTfuXNnp1AIQzHX5Xxoedvk\_-MD1CTRC/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1sTfuXNnp1AIQzHX5Xxoedvk_-MD1CTRC/view?usp=share_link)

2. Slowly move your hips forward, leaning toward the wall. Feel a stretch in your calf muscle in your back leg.   
3. Hold for 30 seconds. Then, switch legs and repeat with the other leg. That is 1 set.

![][image40]

[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1vbJ7fMyE7w4vS3oX\_HrYbKmWuex8NpLR/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1vbJ7fMyE7w4vS3oX_HrYbKmWuex8NpLR/view?usp=share_link)

(headline) Schedule

* Do 1-3 sets of this exercise today.   
* Repeat daily for optimal results.

\<button\> Log exercise

---

---

### Session  7 {#session-7}

**Session title:** 

###### Strengthening Exercises {#strengthening-exercises}

**Session description:**

Practice exercises to strengthen your back and other supporting muscles.  

##### S7M1 {#s7m1}

**Module type:**

Education

**Audio:**  
Yes

**Module title:** 

#### Strength Training to Reduce Pain {#strength-training-to-reduce-pain}

#### **Body:**  {#body:-1}

These strengthening exercises help you maintain a reduced level of lower back pain, as well as prevent future tightness and discomfort.

(headline) Chair squats  
Lifting objects incorrectly can hurt your lower back. Chair squats not only support your back by strengthening your quadricep muscles, they help build muscle memory for the correct lifting technique.

(headline) Crunches  
Crunches help your back by strengthening your abdominal muscles. This exercise focuses directly on working the abdominal muscles — rather than traditional sit-ups which can strain lower back muscles.

(headline) Superman holds  
This exercise strengthens your lower back and core muscles while also building their endurance. You use both abdominal and lower back muscles to support your upper torso.

(headline) Bridges  
Bridges are a face up, backward bending exercise. They stretch and strengthen your back, core, gluteal and hamstring muscles.

\<button\> Mark as done

---

##### S7M2 {#s7m2}

**Module type:**

Physical Exercises

**Audio:**  
Yes

**\# Required:**  
1

**Tracking Content:**  
Log at least 1 day

**Module title:** 

###### Chair Squats {#chair-squats}

#### 

**Body:**   
   
![][image41]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1lWTVQUhJNpSXpNyIVkCd1Ply3ZOhqLyr/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1lWTVQUhJNpSXpNyIVkCd1Ply3ZOhqLyr/view?usp=share_link)

1. Stand in front of a chair, as if you were going to sit down in it. Position your feet a couple of inches wider than your hips. Point your feet straight ahead.

![][image42]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1lT88Fk88k\_bhffMCw0bBpC06BGgQSRvt/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1lT88Fk88k_bhffMCw0bBpC06BGgQSRvt/view?usp=share_link)

2. Bend your legs and move toward the ground while also moving your buttocks back. Bend until you touch the chair, then return to the starting position.  
3. Repeat 10 times — That is 1 set.

![][image43]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1pDhizdQB54n-DnuJLHkNptP3QvawQeYl/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1pDhizdQB54n-DnuJLHkNptP3QvawQeYl/view?usp=share_link)

(headline) Schedule

* Do 1-3 sets of this exercise today.   
* Repeat daily to achieve optimal strength.


(headline) Tip  
During the exercise, focus on using your leg muscles. You can also do the exercise without a chair. Simply squat so your legs form a 90 degree angle.

\<button\> Log activity

**Question page:**  
Date:  
\[date picker with validation 40 & 43\]

How many sets of this exercise did you do?

* 1  
* 2  
* 3  
* More than 3

\[Multiple choice, single-select\]

Performing this exercise resulted in: (select all that apply)

* Increasing range of motion  
* Increasing strength  
* Other

\[Multiple choice, multi-select\]

If other, what did you experience? (optional)  
\[open answer with validation 26\]

##### ---

##### 

##### S7M3 {#s7m3}

**Module type:**

Physical Exercises

**Audio:**  
Yes

**\# Required:**  
1

**Tracking content:**  
Log at least 1 day

**Module title:** 

###### Crunches {#crunches}

#### **Body:**  {#body:-2}

1. Lie flat on your back with your knees bent.

![][image44]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1IbbhdLfzAwtw5BPi\_m9lrHGaq6HcLKJV/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1IbbhdLfzAwtw5BPi_m9lrHGaq6HcLKJV/view?usp=share_link)

2. Tighten your abdominal muscles to lift your head, neck and shoulders upward and toward your knees. 

![][image45]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1QMN0k\_jbq0J8n5yBodiwZvx37cpMcy45/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1QMN0k_jbq0J8n5yBodiwZvx37cpMcy45/view?usp=share_link)

3. Lower your head back to the starting position.  
4. Repeat 5-10 times — That is 1 set.

![][image46]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1ix82oN9U6tJFUD8rRxKKyd9mFwyhPzuk/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1ix82oN9U6tJFUD8rRxKKyd9mFwyhPzuk/view?usp=share_link)

(headline) Schedule

* Do 1-3 sets of this exercise today.   
* Repeat daily to achieve optimal strength.

(headline) Tip  
You can optionally support your neck with a towel. Roll a towel lengthwise, place it behind your neck and grab the towel near your ears. Use your abdominals to initiate the movement. Do not pull your neck with your arms.

\<button\> Log activity

**Question page:**  
Date:  
\[date picker with validation 40 & 43\]

How many sets of this exercise did you do?

* 1  
* 2  
* 3  
* More than 3

\[multiple choice, single-select\]

Performing this exercise resulted in: (select all that apply)

* Increasing range of motion  
* Increasing strength  
* Other

\[Multiple choice, multi-select\]

If other, what did you experience? (optional)  
\[open answer with validation 26\]

---

##### S7M4 {#s7m4}

**Module type:**

Physical Exercises

**Audio:**  
Yes

**\# Required:**  
1

**Tracking content:**  
Log at least 1 day

**Module title:** 

###### Superman Holds {#superman-holds}

#### 

#### **Body:**  {#body:-3}

1. Lie face down on the floor. Place your hands at your sides.

![][image47]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1mzIGx22Yj1MMMMjEFQN6vycpEJb30uoU/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1mzIGx22Yj1MMMMjEFQN6vycpEJb30uoU/view?usp=share_link)

2. Gently lift your upper torso, legs and your hands off the floor at the same time (keeping your knees straight).

![][image48]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1-Byl3HmZ0acXmYX7TsJgH1OJjO8kpdmB/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1-Byl3HmZ0acXmYX7TsJgH1OJjO8kpdmB/view?usp=share_link)

3. Hold for 3-30 seconds. Then return to the starting position.  
4. Repeat 5-10 times — That is 1 set.

![][image49]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1dIeUpJHfn4CtHkTSHIVtkQMYw6ZgdV8J/view?usp=sharing](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1dIeUpJHfn4CtHkTSHIVtkQMYw6ZgdV8J/view?usp=sharing)

(headline) Schedule

* Do 1-3 sets of this exercise today.   
* Repeat daily to achieve optimal strength.


(headline) Tip  
Work on holding the elevated position for longer periods of time. When you can hold the position for 60 seconds or longer, your lower back and core muscles have strong endurance.

\<button\> Log activity

**Question page:**

Date:  
\[date picker with validation 40 & 43\]

How many sets of this exercise did you do?

* 1  
* 2  
* 3  
* More than 3

\[Multiple choice, single-select\]

Performing this exercise resulted in: (select all that apply)

* Increasing range of motion  
* Increasing strength  
* Other

\[Multiple choice, multi-select\]

If other, what did you experience? (optional)  
\[open answer with validation 26\]

##### S7M5 {#s7m5}

**Module type:**

Physical Exercises

**Audio:**  
Yes

**\# Required:**  
1

**Tracking content:**  
Log at least 1 day

**Module title:** 

###### Bridges {#bridges}

#### **Body:**  {#body:-4}

1. Lie on your back on the floor with knees bent. Place your hands at your sides with palms down, touching the ground.

![][image50]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1l-iXGp3v4GN0f9CUTUc3v51k77VJUDNB/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1l-iXGp3v4GN0f9CUTUc3v51k77VJUDNB/view?usp=share_link)

2. Gently lift your hips and upper torso by pushing your feet into the floor. Maintain your hand position on the floor.

![][image51]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1gkLWND7np4ddYspho3lRQLdJR3ahsHI-/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1gkLWND7np4ddYspho3lRQLdJR3ahsHI-/view?usp=share_link)

3. Hold for 3-30 seconds. Then lower back down to the starting position.  
4. Repeat 5-10 times — That is 1 set.

![][image52]  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1T9JjG3DE9ukwjteWMcNVa0kpnvsoaQsS/view?usp=share\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1T9JjG3DE9ukwjteWMcNVa0kpnvsoaQsS/view?usp=share_link)

##### 

(headline) Schedule

* Do 1-3 sets of this exercise today.   
* Repeat daily to achieve optimal strength.

\<button\> Log activity

**Question page:**

* 1  
* 2  
* 3  
* More than 3

Performing this exercise resulted in: (select all that apply)

* Increasing range of motion  
* Increasing strength  
* Other

\[Multiple choice, multi-select\]

If other, what did you experience? (optional)

##### Session 8 {#session-8}

**Session title:** 

###### Maintaining Back Health {#maintaining-back-health}

**Session description:** 

#### Reassess your goals and pain level, and make a plan for moving forward.  {#reassess-your-goals-and-pain-level,-and-make-a-plan-for-moving-forward.}

### 

### S8M1 {#s8m1}

**Module type:**  
Education

**Audio:**  
Yes

**Module title:** 

#### Maintain Your Back Health {#maintain-your-back-health}

**Body:**

In order to maintain your back health, keep practicing healthy sitting and standing postures. Set reminders to check your posture throughout the day. Perform backward and forward bending exercises as necessary.

* Backward bending while standing after sitting for a while.  
* Forward bending when you notice a reduction in your range of motion in that direction.

(headline) Acute back pain  
When acute back pain occurs, lie face down and relax your back muscles, as you practiced in {{session}} 4\. Sit as little as possible and only for short periods of time. Avoid activities that risk stressing your lower back more, such as lifting heavy objects. Contact your healthcare provider if pain is extreme or persists. 

(headline) Continue exercises  
Perform the following exercises regularly, from a few times a week to every other day. Use the correct form to get the most benefits from these exercises. Review this program any time for reference. 

* Hamstring stretch  
* Quadricep stretch  
* Calf stretch  
* Chair squats  
* Crunches  
* Superman holds  
* Bridges

(headline) Key insights

1. Perform backward and forward bending exercises when you notice a decrease in your back’s range of motion.  
2. When acute back pain occurs, lie face down and relax your back muscles. Avoid sitting for long periods and chores that stress your back.  
3. Continue your back strengthening exercises.

\<button\> Mark as done 

#### ---

### S8M2 {#s8m2}

**Module type:**  
Reflection

**Audio:**  
No

**Module title:** 

#### Reflect on Your Goals {#reflect-on-your-goals}

**Body:**

As you complete this program, review your goals and report your current pain level.

\<button\> Reflect

**Question page:**

What is your current pain level on a scale of 0-10? (0-none, 10-severe)  
\[number with validation 28\]

Which goal(s) did you make progress with? (select all that apply)

* Reduce your pain  
* Prevent future pain  
* Increase your back’s range of motion  
* Perform an activity without discomfort  
* None of these

\[multiple choice, multi-select\]

---

### S8M3 {#s8m3}

**Module type:**  
Assessment

**Audio:**  
No

**Module title:** 

#### Reassess Your Back Pain {#reassess-your-back-pain}

**Body:**

(headline) Assessing your discomfort  
Now that you’ve completed the exercises in this program, identify your current level of discomfort based on any pain you feel in your lower back. This will help you assess your improvement.

\<button\> Take assessment

**Question page:** 

How difficult do you find it today to **get out of bed?**

* Not difficult at all (0)  
* Minimally difficult (1)  
* Somewhat difficult (2)  
* Fairly difficult (3)  
* Very difficult (4)  
* Unable to do (5)

\[multiple choice, choose one\]

How difficult do you find it today to **sleep through the night?**

* Not difficult at all (0)  
* Minimally difficult (1)  
* Somewhat difficult (2)  
* Fairly difficult (3)  
* Very difficult (4)  
* Unable to do (5)

\[multiple choice, choose one\]

How difficult do you find it today to **turn over in bed?**

* Not difficult at all (0)  
* Minimally difficult (1)  
* Somewhat difficult (2)  
* Fairly difficult (3)  
* Very difficult (4)  
* Unable to do (5)

\[multiple choice, choose one\]

How difficult do you find it today to **ride in a car?**

* Not difficult at all (0)  
* Minimally difficult (1)  
* Somewhat difficult (2)  
* Fairly difficult (3)  
* Very difficult (4)  
* Unable to do (5)

\[multiple choice, choose one\]

How difficult do you find it today to **stand up for 20-30 minutes?**

* Not difficult at all (0)  
* Minimally difficult (1)  
* Somewhat difficult (2)  
* Fairly difficult (3)  
* Very difficult (4)  
* Unable to do (5)

\[multiple choice, choose one\]

How difficult do you find it today to **sit in a chair for several hours?**

* Not difficult at all (0)  
* Minimally difficult (1)  
* Somewhat difficult (2)  
* Fairly difficult (3)  
* Very difficult (4)  
* Unable to do (5)

\[multiple choice, choose one\]

How difficult do you find it today to **climb one flight of stairs?**

* Not difficult at all (0)

* Somewhat difficult (2)  
* Fairly difficult (3)  
* Very difficult (4)  
* Unable to do (5)

\[multiple choice, choose one\]

How difficult do you find it today to **walk a few blocks?**

* Not difficult at all (0)  
* Minimally difficult (1)  
* Somewhat difficult (2)  
* Fairly difficult (3)  
* Very difficult (4)  
* Unable to do (5)

\[multiple choice, choose one\]

How difficult do you find it today to **walk several miles?**

* Not difficult at all (0)  
* Minimally difficult (1)  
* Somewhat difficult (2)  
* Fairly difficult (3)  
* Very difficult (4)  
* Unable to do (5)

\[multiple choice, choose one\]

How difficult do you find it today to **reach up to high shelves?**

* Not difficult at all (0)

* Somewhat difficult (2)  
* Fairly difficult (3)  
* Very difficult (4)  
* Unable to do (5)

\[multiple choice, choose one\]

* Not difficult at all (0)

* Somewhat difficult (2)  
* Fairly difficult (3)  
* Very difficult (4)  
* Unable to do (5)

\[multiple choice, choose one\]

How difficult do you find it today to **run one block?**

* Not difficult at all (0)  
* Minimally difficult (1)  
* Somewhat difficult (2)  
* Fairly difficult (3)  
* Very difficult (4)  
* Unable to do (5)

\[multiple choice, choose one\]

How difficult do you find it today to **take food out of the refrigerator?**

* Not difficult at all (0)

\[multiple choice, choose one\]

### S8M4 {#s8m4}

**Module type:**  
Care plan

**Audio:**  
No

**Module title:** 

#### Moving Forward {#moving-forward}

**Body:**

Reflect on what aspects of the program worked best for you and make a care plan for what you will do moving forward.

If your pain continues to worsen or severely impact your daily life, contact your healthcare provider for additional support.

\<button\> Create plan

**Question page:**

Based on what helped you most, which **posture techniques** will you continue to do? (select all that apply)

* Sitting  
* Standing  
* Lifting  
* None

\[Multiple choice, multi-select\]

How often will you do them? (optional)

* Daily  
* 3 times a week  
* Once a week  
* Other

\[Multiple choice, single-select\]

Based on what helped you most, which **backward bending exercises** will you continue to do? (select all that apply)

* Press up to elbows   
* Press up to straight arms   
* Standing backward bend  
* None

\[Multiple choice, multi-select\]

How often will you do them? (optional)

* Daily  
* 3 times a week  
* Once a week  
* Other

\[Multiple choice, single-select\]

Based on what helped you most, which **forward bending exercises** will you continue to do? (select all that apply) 

* Lying forward bend   
* Standing forward bend  
* None

\[Multiple choice, multi-select\]

How often will you do them? (optional)

* Daily  
* 3 times a week  
* Once a week  
* Other

\[Multiple choice, single-select\]

Based on what helped you most, which **stretches** will you continue to do? (select all that apply) 

* Hamstring   
* Quadricep   
* Calf  
* None

\[Multiple choice, multi-select\]

How often will you do them? (optional)

* Daily  
* 3 times a week  
* Once a week  
* Other

\[Multiple choice, single-select\]

Based on what helped you most, which **strengthening exercises** will you continue to do? (select all that apply)

* Chair squats  
* Crunches  
* Superman holds  
* Bridges  
* None

\[Multiple choice, multi-select\]

How often will you do them? (optional)

* Daily  
* 3 times a week  
* Once a week  
* Other

\[Multiple choice, single-select\]

If you have an acute pain/flare up, what will you do? (select all that apply)

* "Relax your back" exercise  
* Contact your healthcare provider  
* Other

If other, what will you do? (optional)  
\[open answer with validation 26\]
